summary: Descriptive overview of zoninglanduse in substantive law: Euclidean zoning, variances, takings implications.
key_points: Euclidean zoning is a fundamental concept in property law.
Legal standards for zoninglanduse are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine zoninglanduse in litigation.
The doctrine of zoninglanduse has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding zoninglanduse requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
sources: Supreme Court opinions (Kelo v. New London)
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
